title.rendered: AMI at Embedded World 2015 in Nuremberg, Germany: February 24 – 26, 2015

content.rendered:
NORCROSS, GEORGIA, USA / NUREMBERG, GERMANY – AMI, Inc., (AMI) a global leader in BIOS, remote management and network data storage solutions, joins Embedded World 2015 from Tuesday, February 24 to Thursday, February 26, 2015 in Nuremberg, Germany.

The Embedded World Exhibition & Conference is the world’s biggest exhibition of its kind and is a key meeting place of the international embedded community, shaping the character of the embedded world by showcasing the full range of embedded technologies in hardware, software, tools and development services.

AMI will be located in Hall 2, Booth 365 of the Exhibition Centre Nuremberg, where the company will display a number of exciting embedded solutions for the emerging Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystem. As one of the highlights of this year’s presentation, AMI will unveil its vision for intelligent management of IoT devices with demonstrations of its cloud-centric IoT gateway solution, which combines key components from widely-used, mature AMI products and leverages its deep experience in BIOS, remote management and sensor management technologies.

And as always, AMI will also present the latest developments in its flagship Aptio® V UEFI BIOS Firmware, together with BIOS development and debug tools and services, showing how both x86 and ARM-based platforms can go “beyond BIOS” using Unified Extensible Firmware Interface (UEFI) technology to add value and reduce time-to-market (TTM) for embedded projects. Solutions for the Android™ OS for x86-based hardware and MegaRAC® Remote Management products will be on display as well.

AMI warmly welcomes its customers, partners and media representatives to the Embedded World Exhibition & Conference and invites attendees to visit Booth 365 in Hall 2 to witness the latest in AMI innovation for the embedded market.
